vulnerabilities.description: CPF stored in public 'users' document. Any user could read the encrypted CPF of any other user.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Move CPF and sensitive fields to a restricted subcollection '/users/{uid}/private/sensitive' and restrict read access to the owner and admins.

vulnerabilities.description: Encryption key hardcoded in client-side accessible file (crypto-utils.ts). Key could be extracted from JS bundle.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Encryption/Decryption logic restricted to Server Actions. Cryptographic keys stored as environment variables.

vulnerabilities.description: Registrations collection allowed read access to any signed-in user, exposing attendee data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Restrict registration read access to the buyer, the attendee, organization members, or global admins.

vulnerabilities.description: Financial snapshots in registrations potentially exposed sensitive fee breakdowns to public users.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Mask sensitive financial data in public views.

architecture_updates.access_policy: Public discovery maintained; Sensitive data isolated and server-side decrypted.
